name: Pulsar
description: Radio beacons ringing out into deep space — twin shockwaves widen around each bright core, re-emitted on every redraw.

code.style: --rule: ( /* wave and core share one emitted signal color; the faint outer wave is the ring's spread shadow */ --sig: @p(var(--color1), var(--color1), var(--color2), var(--color3)); @random(${shapeFrequency}) { :before { content: ''; position: absolute; inset: @rand(12%, 26%); border: calc(18px / @Y) solid @var(--sig); border-radius: 50%; opacity: @rand(0.35, 0.7); box-shadow: 0 0 0 calc(@r(40, 90) * 1px / @Y) rgba(242, 244, 255, 0.1); box-sizing: border-box; transition: ease 700ms; } :after { content: ''; position: absolute; @size: @rand(7%, 12%); left: 46%; top: 46%; border-radius: 50%; background: @var(--sig); box-shadow: 0 0 calc(130px / @Y) @var(--sig); transition: ease 700ms; } } );
code.doodle: :doodle { @grid: ${grid}; @size: ${width} ${height}; overflow:hidden; text-align:center; box-sizing:border-box } :container { background: var(--color0); overflow:hidden; }
